title: One-Pot Pasta with Tomatoes & Spinach
description: A quick and easy one-pot pasta dish packed with fresh tomatoes and spinach, perfect for a busy weeknight family meal. It's simple to make and kid-friendly!
instructions: Halve the cherry tomatoes and finely mince the garlic cloves.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine the halved cherry tomatoes, minced garlic, vegetable broth, uncooked spaghetti (break in half if needed), olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, stirring occasionally to prevent the pasta from sticking.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pot, and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until the pasta is al dente and most of the liquid has been absorbed. Stir occasionally.
Stir in the fresh spinach and cook for another 2-3 minutes, or until the spinach has wilted.
Remove from heat and let it sit for a few minutes to allow any remaining liquid to be absorbed.
Serve immediately, garnished with optional Parmesan cheese.
tips: For extra flavor, you can add a pinch of red pepper flakes with the other seasonings.
If the pasta seems too dry, add a splash more vegetable broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave, adding a little water or broth if needed.
